roadmap.name: RoadForge Public Alpha Readiness + GitHub Links

tasks.title: Audit large files and define refactor boundaries
tasks.desc:
Create a read-only map of current large frontend/backend files and where responsibilities are mixed.


**Scope**
- Identify files that act as orchestration hubs, especially Workspace, TaskRow, save/sync hooks, roadmap services, router modules, and CSS ownership.
- Mark what should be split now versus deferred until after alpha.
- Document invariants that must not break: local-first cache, whole-task lock safety, autosync, import/export compatibility, role gates.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] File responsibility map exists in docs/architecture or an implementation note.
- [ ] Each proposed split has a concrete target module and rollback-safe slice.
- [ ] No code changes in this audit slice.


**Validation**
`pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build` if docs imports or references are touched; otherwise review only.

tasks.title: Split Workspace orchestration into focused containers and hooks
tasks.desc:
Reduce Workspace.tsx from a central conductor into smaller ownership-based pieces.


**Scope**
- Extract view/panel orchestration, participant/team operations, restore/version panel coordination, and task mutation wiring only where it reduces complexity.
- Preserve existing route behavior and localStorage UI state behavior.
- Do not change product behavior.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Workspace becomes easier to scan and does not own unrelated panel internals.
- [ ] Roadmap/Team/Activity/Versions behavior remains unchanged.
- [ ] No new monolithic replacement file is introduced.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`.

tasks.title: Split TaskRow into header, description, metadata, actions, and child sections
tasks.desc:
TaskRow still carries too much UI and state logic. Split it into small components without changing behavior.


**Scope**
- Keep title inline editing and description inline editing intact.
- Keep Estimate, Assignees, and Tags as read-only metadata edited via Edit details.
- Keep Add subtask, Link dependency, claim row, dependencies, subtasks, locks, and read-only states intact.
- Avoid introducing a new all-purpose TaskDetail monolith.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] TaskRow becomes a composition shell with clear subcomponents.
- [ ] Each extracted file has a narrow responsibility.
- [ ] No regression in expanded task behavior.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`; manual check one expanded task.

tasks.title: Split save, autosync, conflict, and realtime status ownership
tasks.desc:
Make save/sync/conflict behavior easier to maintain before adding GitHub link mutations.


**Scope**
- Separate debounced autosync, explicit save, conflict review, session expiry, and activity refresh responsibilities where practical.
- Keep existing conflict behavior and draft preservation.
- Do not change backend APIs.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Autosync and explicit save flows are easier to test independently.
- [ ] Conflict review still opens for 409 metadata.
- [ ] Offline/session-expired behavior remains unchanged.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`; manual conflict smoke if touched.

tasks.title: Clarify RoadmapContext and remove legacy combined usage where practical
tasks.desc:
RoadmapContext is already split internally, but many consumers may still use the legacy combined hook.


**Scope**
- Prefer focused hooks for new/refactored components.
- Remove legacy combined usage only where it is safe and improves subscription clarity.
- Keep the legacy hook exported until all major consumers are migrated.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] No component subscribes to more roadmap state than it needs in newly touched areas.
- [ ] No behavior change in hydration or realtime subscription.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`.

tasks.title: Modularize workspace CSS by component ownership
tasks.desc:
Reduce CSS sprawl by moving task-detail, toolbar, editor, and panel rules into ownership-based files.


**Scope**
- Do not rename public class names unless every usage is updated in the same slice.
- Prefer additive cleanup with before/after screenshots.
- Keep mobile rules close to their owning component when possible.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] CSS files are easier to navigate.
- [ ] Expanded task detail remains visually unchanged or improved.
- [ ] No broad global resets are introduced.


**Validation**
`pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`; manual desktop/mobile screenshot check.

tasks.title: Write architecture decision records for source-of-truth rules
tasks.desc:
Document the rules that protect RoadForge from turning into an issue tracker or fragile sync layer.


**Scope**
- RoadForge owns roadmap intent, sequencing, and task planning.
- GitHub owns implementation artifacts.
- Imports/exports must stay portable and credential-free.
- Task links are references, not automatic status drivers.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] ADR exists and is referenced by GitHub implementation tasks.
- [ ] Future agents have clear do/don't guidance.


**Validation**
Review only; no runtime validation required.

phases.name: Frontend state and task UX consolidation

tasks.title: Finalize compact expanded task detail layout
tasks.desc:
Consolidate the current task detail UX after the metadata regression patch.


**Scope**
- Description inline editing remains available without a noisy text button.
- Estimate, Assignees, and Tags are compact read-only metadata edited through Edit details.
- Action buttons are compact and visually integrated.
- Do one last desktop and mobile visual pass.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] No large full-width metadata boxes.
- [ ] No inline metadata inputs for estimate/assignees/tags.
- [ ] Edit details reliably opens TaskEditForm and preserves lock behavior.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`; browser check at desktop and 600px.

tasks.title: Confirm task edit mode coverage after metadata simplification
tasks.desc:
Audit which fields are edited inline versus via Edit details and remove dead inline metadata code.


**Scope**
- Title and description remain inline.
- Estimate, assignees, tags use Edit details.
- Remove unused TaskTagEditor/TaskAssigneeEditor/TagInput props only if no remaining imports need them.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] No dead imports or unused files.
- [ ] TaskEditForm remains the canonical metadata editor.
- [ ] Tests updated if helpers were simplified.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`.

tasks.title: Tighten task lock UX around Edit details and description editing
tasks.desc:
Make lock behavior obvious but not noisy.


**Scope**
- Edit details, title edit, description edit, Add subtask, and Link dependency all use the same whole-task lock rules.
- Other-user lock shows a concise hint.
- Idle pause never softlocks the team.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Second user cannot enter edit flows while lock is active.
- [ ] Idle release lets another user eventually edit.
- [ ] Original user's draft is not silently lost.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`; two-browser manual QA.

tasks.title: Add focused helper tests for task metadata and description editor behavior
tasks.desc:
Backfill tests for the areas most likely to regress during refactors.


**Scope**
- Pure tests for task metadata display helpers if introduced.
- Description editor behavior tests only if current test setup supports component tests cleanly.
- Avoid brittle screenshot-style tests.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Description draft preservation, dirty cancel, and keyboard save are covered where practical.
- [ ] Metadata simplification does not regress import/export fields.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test` plus full web validation.

tasks.title: Normalize activity labels for task updates and metadata edits
tasks.desc:
Activity should remain useful after the editing model changed.


**Scope**
- Ensure title/description/Edit details changes generate understandable activity.
- Avoid activity spam for no-op saves.
- Do not introduce a new activity model unless necessary.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Activity panel distinguishes meaningful task updates.
- [ ] No duplicate rows for a single user action.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`; manual Activity panel check.

tasks.title: Clean up local UI state persistence boundaries
tasks.desc:
Ensure per-roadmap UI state stores only UI preferences, not source-of-truth data.


**Scope**
- Expanded task, open phases, dismissed notices belong in UI state.
- Roadmap data, auth, and session state stay separate.
- Parse errors and stale state remain safe.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Storage schema remains backward compatible.
- [ ] Invalid UI state does not crash hydration.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test -- src/lib/__tests__/storage.test.ts && pnpm typecheck`.

phases.name: Backend write model, realtime, and data integrity hardening

tasks.title: Design partial task write API shape before implementation
tasks.desc:
Define the partial task write model to reduce full-snapshot conflicts.


**Scope**
- Document endpoint shape, optimistic concurrency, activity metadata, version policy, SSE event behavior, and validation limits.
- Use existing task done/claim routes as the style reference.
- Keep local-first import/export model unchanged.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Design is small enough to implement in reviewable slices.
- [ ] No task field can be silently overwritten by stale clients.


**Validation**
Review only; no runtime validation required.

tasks.title: Add task-level PATCH endpoint for title, description, and metadata
tasks.desc:
Implement a focused task PATCH endpoint for scalar task fields and metadata.


**Scope**
- Owner/editor only.
- Accept title, desc, est, assignees, tags plus required last_updated_at.
- Validate with existing limits and normalized helpers.
- Return full RoadmapResponse for frontend compatibility.
- Emit task.updated activity and roadmap.updated SSE.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Partial update touches only provided task fields.
- [ ] Stale last_updated_at returns 409 conflict metadata.
- [ ] Viewer is 403; owner/editor are 200.
- [ ] Tests cover title, desc, est, tags, assignees.


**Validation**
Run full apps/api pytest plus web typecheck.

tasks.title: Route synced title and description commits through task PATCH
tasks.desc:
Stop using full roadmap PUT for title and description commits on synced roadmaps.


**Scope**
- Local roadmaps keep current local mutation path.
- Synced roadmaps call task PATCH and apply returned roadmap/updatedAt.
- Conflict feeds existing conflict handling while preserving draft state.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Single title/description edit issues one task PATCH.
- [ ] No full PUT for these edits.
- [ ] Offline/session-expired/conflict paths remain recoverable.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`; two-session conflict QA.

tasks.title: Keep Edit details metadata writes safe with the new PATCH path
tasks.desc:
Route Estimate, Assignees, and Tags through task PATCH when edited via Edit details on synced roadmaps.


**Scope**
- Keep TaskEditForm UI unchanged.
- No reintroduction of inline metadata editing.
- No-op saves should not generate activity.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Edit details updates metadata without full roadmap PUT on synced roadmaps.
- [ ] Tag registry behavior remains compatible.
- [ ] Local roadmaps still work without backend.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`; API focused tests.

tasks.title: Projection-read readiness and drift tests
tasks.desc:
Make the relational projection trustworthy before considering it for reads.


**Scope**
- Verify projection matches snapshot for phases, tasks, tags, deps, subtasks, claim fields.
- Add drift tests around create/update/restore/import flows.
- Do not flip production reads yet unless the test evidence is strong.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Projection drift test fails if snapshot and relational projection diverge.
- [ ] Restore/import paths included.


**Validation**
Run full apps/api pytest.

tasks.title: Redis-backed realtime deployment check
tasks.desc:
Confirm the deployed realtime mode is safe for current worker count and future scaling.


**Scope**
- Document memory vs Redis realtime behavior.
- Verify startup guard prevents unsafe multi-worker memory mode.
- Run a deploy smoke with Redis mode if target stack supports it.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Realtime docs match environment variables.
- [ ] No multi-worker unsafe config can start silently.


**Validation**
API tests plus deployment smoke.

tasks.title: Restore automatic CI triggers before external alpha sharing
tasks.desc:
CI is currently manual-only. Restore push/PR triggers before wider alpha use.


**Scope**
- Keep workflow_dispatch.
- Add push/pull_request triggers for main and active PR branches.
- Avoid expensive jobs on irrelevant docs-only changes only if simple.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Every PR runs web, API, migration, and audit gates.
- [ ] Manual dispatch remains available.


**Validation**
Open a test PR or workflow dispatch and verify jobs.

tasks.title: Add one-command full local release gate
tasks.desc:
Make the final release check hard to forget.


**Scope**
- One command should run web tests, lint, typecheck, build, API lint, API tests, migration check where practical, and diff check notes.
- Document expected runtime and failure handling.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Release gate command exists and is documented.
- [ ] It matches what we manually run today.


**Validation**
Run the new command locally.

tasks.title: Backfill tests for lock, idle, and partial-write conflict paths
tasks.desc:
Cover the collaboration paths most likely to regress.


**Scope**
- Lock acquire/release/idle pause.
- Task PATCH 409 conflict.
- Description draft preservation on conflict where practical.
- Viewer/editor/owner permission matrix.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Automated tests cover lock and conflict basics.
- [ ] Manual QA checklist remains for browser-only behavior.


**Validation**
Run full web tests and full API tests.

tasks.title: Maximal import/export round-trip fixture
tasks.desc:
Add a compatibility fixture that catches missed task fields.


**Scope**
- Markdown desc with lists/checkboxes/links.
- Assignees, tags, tag registry colors.
- Deps, subtasks, claim fields.
- Future task.links once GitHub v1 lands.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Round trip preserves fields byte-for-byte where intended.
- [ ] Test fails when a new task field lacks import/export support.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test`.

tasks.title: Manual browser smoke checklist automation candidate
tasks.desc:
Identify the minimal browser automation worth adding before alpha.


**Scope**
- Owner/editor/viewer join flow.
- Expanded task edit flow.
- Import/export replace checkpoint.
- Version read/restore owner-only.
- Realtime update between two sessions if automation setup supports it.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Recommendation written: Playwright now or after alpha.
- [ ] If implemented, it covers only high-value smoke paths.


**Validation**
Review only or run browser smoke tests if implemented.

tasks.title: Release checklist document for Public Alpha candidate
tasks.desc:
Prepare the checklist used in the final alpha QA phase.


**Scope**
- Local validation commands.
- Deploy commands.
- Owner/editor/viewer browser QA.
- Rollback/backup checks.
- Known limitations.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Final QA can be executed once at the end without rediscovering the process.
- [ ] Checklist references current product behavior.


**Validation**
Review only.

tasks.title: Proxy and app log review for invite-token leakage
tasks.desc:
Invite tokens are URL credentials. Confirm logs do not expose them casually.


**Scope**
- Review nginx/proxy access logs.
- Review app logs and browser error paths.
- Document safe logging guidance.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Join URLs are not written to public/shared logs.
- [ ] Operational doc explains remaining risk.


**Validation**
Manual server log review plus docs update.

tasks.title: Database backup and restore drill
tasks.desc:
Prove recovery before sharing alpha more widely.


**Scope**
- Take a Postgres backup.
- Restore into a disposable DB or container.
- Verify roadmaps, participants, versions, and activity exist.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Backup command documented.
- [ ] Restore command documented.
- [ ] One real restore drill completed.


**Validation**
Manual ops validation.

tasks.title: Move CSP from report-only plan toward enforceable policy
tasks.desc:
Review CSP violations and decide what can be enforced safely.


**Scope**
- Do not break Next.js assets or current Markdown rendering.
- Keep report-only if evidence is insufficient.
- Document any required exceptions.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Decision recorded.
- [ ] If enforced, browser smoke passes.


**Validation**
`pnpm build` and browser QA if policy changes.

tasks.title: Invite and session expiry review
tasks.desc:
Review accountless sharing risk before GitHub links increase team usage.


**Scope**
- Owner/editor/viewer token lifecycle.
- Rotate/revoke behavior.
- Participant session expiry.
- Public viewer link expectations.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Docs and UI warnings match actual behavior.
- [ ] No route grants more than intended.


**Validation**
API auth/share-link tests.

tasks.title: Operational monitoring and health check polish
tasks.desc:
Make hosted alpha easier to monitor.


**Scope**
- Health endpoint and reverse proxy behavior.
- Container restart/update behavior.
- Basic error log checklist.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Health check proves API is up.
- [ ] Deploy/runbook includes what to inspect when RoadForge is down.


**Validation**
Manual deploy smoke.

phases.name: GitHub links v1 - URL-only source-of-truth layer

tasks.title: Design task external-link model and GitHub source-of-truth rules
tasks.desc:
Define the first GitHub layer without credentials or bidirectional sync.


**Scope**
- RoadForge task owns planning intent.
- GitHub issue/PR/discussion is linked work evidence.
- No automatic task completion from GitHub state.
- No credentials in exports/imports/localStorage.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] TaskExternalLink shape is documented.
- [ ] URL-only v1 behavior is clear.
- [ ] Future metadata cache is explicitly separate from roadmap truth.


**Validation**
Review only.

tasks.title: Add task.links import/export and validation support
tasks.desc:
Add portable task link storage to roadmap JSON.


**Scope**
- Support provider='github' and kind issue/pull/discussion/commit/release/url.
- Keep links credential-free.
- Preserve links through import/export round trips.
- Unknown/invalid links repair safely.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Import/export preserves task.links.
- [ ] Existing roadmaps without links still import cleanly.
- [ ] Maximal fixture includes links.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`.

tasks.title: Build GitHub URL parser and normalizer
tasks.desc:
Parse pasted GitHub URLs into stable task link references.


**Scope**
- Support issue, pull request, discussion, commit, release, and generic GitHub URL forms.
- Reject non-GitHub URLs unless explicitly attached as generic URL later.
- Normalize owner/repo/number/kind.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Parser has table-driven tests.
- [ ] Malformed URLs return friendly errors.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test`.

tasks.title: Add Link GitHub modal for pasted URLs
tasks.desc:
Let users attach GitHub work to a RoadForge task with the simplest possible UX.


**Scope**
- Button: Link GitHub.
- Paste URL input.
- Preview parsed type and repo.
- Attach as URL-only link.
- No GitHub account connection yet.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Editor/owner can attach links.
- [ ] Viewer cannot attach links.
- [ ] Works on local roadmaps and synced roadmaps.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`; browser QA.

tasks.title: Render Linked work cards in expanded task detail
tasks.desc:
Show attached GitHub work as compact cards in task detail.


**Scope**
- Cards show kind, repo, number/hash, title fallback from URL if metadata unavailable.
- Click opens GitHub in a new tab.
- Collapsed rows show only a subtle linked-work count/icon if useful.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Linked work is visible but does not dominate task detail.
- [ ] Empty state is quiet.


**Validation**
`pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`; visual browser check.

tasks.title: Add/remove GitHub links through task lock and partial write safety
tasks.desc:
Make task links collaborative-safe.


**Scope**
- Use whole-task lock before add/remove.
- On synced roadmaps use task PATCH if implemented; otherwise use safe current mutation with conflict handling.
- Remove link requires confirmation only if accidental deletion risk is high.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Two-session add/remove does not silently overwrite.
- [ ] Activity records task link changes clearly.
- [ ] Realtime updates other collaborators.


**Validation**
Full web/API validation plus two-session manual QA.

tasks.title: Manual QA and docs for GitHub links v1
tasks.desc:
Document GitHub URL-only linking and what it deliberately does not do.


**Scope**
- How to attach issue/PR/discussion URLs.
- RoadForge remains source of truth.
- No automatic sync or task completion.
- Export/import behavior.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Docs match UI.
- [ ] Manual QA checklist covers links.


**Validation**
Review plus browser QA.

phases.name: GitHub public metadata and private-repo integration design

tasks.title: Design metadata cache separate from roadmap source of truth
tasks.desc:
Prevent volatile GitHub state from polluting roadmap snapshots.


**Scope**
- Metadata cache stores title/state/labels/assignees/checks separately.
- Task links remain stable references.
- Exports do not include cache or credentials.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Cache schema and invalidation rules are documented.
- [ ] No source-of-truth ambiguity.


**Validation**
Review only.

tasks.title: Public GitHub metadata fetch endpoint
tasks.desc:
Fetch lightweight public metadata for linked GitHub work.


**Scope**
- No authentication required.
- Gracefully handles rate limits, deleted/private repos, and network failures.
- Caches results to avoid repeated requests.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Public issue/PR/discussion cards can show title/state.
- [ ] Private repo links fall back to URL-only until GitHub App exists.


**Validation**
API tests with mocked GitHub responses.

tasks.title: Render GitHub title, state, labels, and assignees on linked-work cards
tasks.desc:
Make linked work more useful without taking over RoadForge.


**Scope**
- Show metadata when available.
- Show stale/fetch-failed state subtly.
- Do not let GitHub state auto-change task done status.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Cards improve context.
- [ ] Fallback remains clean when metadata unavailable.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`.

tasks.title: PR checks and review status summary
tasks.desc:
For PR links, summarize implementation readiness.


**Scope**
- Open/closed/merged/draft state.
- Latest check conclusion.
- Review state if available through public API.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] PR cards show useful status without requiring GitHub login for public repos.
- [ ] Failures are non-blocking UI state, not roadmap errors.


**Validation**
API tests with mocked states; browser card QA.

tasks.title: Linked-work filters
tasks.desc:
Add lightweight filters once metadata exists.


**Scope**
- Has linked work.
- No linked work.
- Open PR / merged PR if metadata is available.
- Failed checks if metadata is available.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Filters do not hide links without metadata incorrectly.
- [ ] Local roadmaps still work.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`.

tasks.title: GitHub App private-repo integration design doc
tasks.desc:
Design the future GitHub App integration before any credentialed implementation.


**Scope**
- Installation-based auth, not PATs in localStorage.
- Roadmap-level connection records.
- Allowed repository scoping.
- No credentials in exports/imports.
- Webhook/cache refresh path.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Security model reviewed before implementation.
- [ ] Implementation split is commit-sized.


**Validation**
Review only.

tasks.title: Evaluate create-GitHub-issue-from-task as a beta feature
tasks.desc:
Decide whether RoadForge should write to GitHub later.


**Scope**
- Assess whether creating issues from tasks helps or harms source-of-truth clarity.
- If yes, define one-way creation only.
- Do not implement in alpha.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Go/no-go decision recorded.
- [ ] No bidirectional sync implied.


**Validation**
Review only.

phases.name: Alpha stabilization and final release QA

tasks.title: Freeze feature work and create alpha release candidate
tasks.desc:
Stop feature changes and produce a single release candidate for final QA.


**Scope**
- Confirm all previous implementation phases are merged and deployed to the target environment.
- Only regression fixes are allowed after this point.
- Record commit SHA and deployment timestamp.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Working tree is clean.
- [ ] Release candidate commit is known.
- [ ] No more planned feature work remains before alpha.


**Validation**
`git status -sb`, full local release gate, then deploy.

tasks.title: Run full owner/editor/viewer browser QA pass once
tasks.desc:
Perform the final end-to-end human QA after all codebase improvements and GitHub v1 work are done.


**Scope**
- Owner create/save/share.
- Editor join/edit/link GitHub work.
- Viewer read-only shared view.
- Import/export/replace/version visibility.
- Mobile sanity pass.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] All critical alpha paths pass.
- [ ] Defects are logged with severity.
- [ ] No repeated QA loop required unless a regression fix lands.


**Validation**
Manual QA using docs/manual-qa.md and release checklist.

tasks.title: Two-session realtime, lock, and GitHub link QA
tasks.desc:
Verify collaboration safety with the new GitHub link layer included.


**Scope**
- Two participants edit different tasks.
- One participant locks a task; the other is blocked gracefully.
- GitHub link add/remove appears live to the other participant.
- Offline/reconnect status remains stable.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] No softlocks.
- [ ] No silent overwrite.
- [ ] Realtime updates arrive without refresh.


**Validation**
Two-browser manual QA.

tasks.title: Final security and deployment smoke
tasks.desc:
Confirm production assumptions before sharing the alpha.


**Scope**
- Health check.
- Reverse proxy/TLS.
- Invite-token logging review.
- Backup exists.
- Rollback notes ready.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Hosted alpha is reachable.
- [ ] Backup/restore procedure is documented.
- [ ] No obvious credential leakage.


**Validation**
Manual ops checklist.

tasks.title: Alpha defect triage and stabilization patch window
tasks.desc:
Patch only defects found during final QA.


**Scope**
- No new features.
- Small commits.
- Each fix gets focused validation.
- If a fix touches a critical flow, rerun only that flow plus smoke.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] All blocking defects resolved or explicitly deferred.
- [ ] No known data-loss/security blocker remains.


**Validation**
Focused validation per patch plus final smoke.

tasks.title: Prepare alpha release notes and known limitations
tasks.desc:
Write what users need to know before they try RoadForge.


**Scope**
- What works.
- What is intentionally alpha.
- GitHub linking limitations.
- Source-available/non-commercial future note.
- Backup/export recommendation.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Release notes are honest and clear.
- [ ] Known limitations do not overpromise.


**Validation**
Review only.
